summary: Iteration 5 — Dashboard latest_entry sync bug fix verified end-to-end. Backend: 41/41 pytest PASSED (10 new in test_latest_entry.py + test_backfill_daily_rest.py covering structure, enrichment, backfill, history-sync). Frontend (420x900, admin@routier-facile.fr): Dashboard 'Dernière journée · MERCREDI 11/02/2026' displays correct values from latest_entry (amplitude 10h00, worked 9h15, driving 7h30, rest 0h45) matching History exactly. New tile today-daily-rest renders '11h30 / OK ≥ 11h'. ComplianceBar indicator-rest reads from latest_entry showing 'OK / 11h30'. History shows both entries with Archive badge on 10/02 (legacy, no cycle_id).
critical_code_review_comments: dashboard_summary now correctly fetches latest_doc across ALL entries (not cycle-scoped) and backfills daily_rest_minutes on-the-fly for legacy docs — clean implementation in /app/backend/server.py:486-504.

Dashboard.jsx isToday check (line 32-36) recomputes today's ISO from local Date — fine, but note: server uses UTC date (timezone.utc) while frontend uses local date, so a session at 23:30 local could mis-label 'Aujourd'hui' vs server's tomorrow. Edge case, low priority.
ComplianceBar.DailyRestTile correctly switched to read latest_entry?.daily_rest_status (line 14) and latest_entry?.daily_rest_minutes (line 40) — fix verified.
Backfill in dashboard_summary mutates latest_doc.daily_rest_minutes in-memory only (not persisted). This is the intended behavior per spec ('on the fly') — no DB writes from a read endpoint. Good.
server.py is still ~600 lines; carry-over from iter3/4 — consider splitting into routers/ when functionality grows.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (dashboard latest_entry tiles + indicator-rest + history Archive badge)

test_credentials: admin@routier-facile.fr / Admin123! (has 2 entries: 2026-02-10 legacy + 2026-02-11 current). Fresh registered users TEST_latest_*, TEST_legacy_*, TEST_bf_*, TEST_solo_* per test run.
seed_data_creation: test_backfill_daily_rest.py seeds 2 legacy entries directly into MongoDB (cycle_id=None, daily_rest_minutes=None) to truly exercise the backfill path. Other tests use API only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter5 confirmed dashboard-history sync. Total pytest count is 41 across 3 files (backend_test.py 19, test_break_rule.py 12, test_latest_entry.py 8, test_backfill_daily_rest.py 2). Admin test data is stable across runs (2 entries). Key testids verified: today-section, today-amplitude, today-worked, today-driving, today-rest, today-daily-rest, indicator-rest. Dashboard label switches between 'Aujourd'hui · <weekday>' (date == today) and 'Dernière journée · <weekday> <date>' (otherwise), with optional Archive badge when latest.is_legacy. Open carry-over from iter4: spec discrepancy on [270,270]/[15,30] break rule case.
rca of the issue: Original bug — Dashboard ComplianceBar read daily_rest from cycle's last_entry which was null when user had no entries in current cycle (only legacy ones). Fix: server.dashboard_summary now also queries latest_doc across ALL entries (no cycle filter), backfills daily_rest_minutes by comparing to previous entry when missing, enriches and exposes as latest_entry. Dashboard.jsx + ComplianceBar.jsx switched to data.latest_entry. Verified: admin (2 entries, mixed legacy+current) now sees correct '11h30 / OK' on both the new today-daily-rest tile and the ComplianceBar 'Repos jour' indicator, matching History page exactly.
